mentions.note: Ultimate consumer staples company; essential products; solid dividend holding.
Ultimate soft goods company; consumer staple with yield; defensive diversification.
Reports Tuesday morning; expect better quarter than P&G; merging with Kleenex in cash-stock deal but warming up to stock after P&G and Clorox rallies.
5%+ yield with fantastic Capital View merger deal expected to produce tremendous returns; stock not rewarded yet but deal fundamentals sound.

Premier consumer staple brands (Kleenex, Kotex, Huggies, Pull-Ups) at 13x earnings, 5.25% dividend yield. Merging with J&J consumer division; Michael Shu (terrific operator) running integration of world-class portfolio.
Weakness tied more to the Kenvue acquisition overhang than the core business; intriguing at 13x earnings with a 5% yield.
Defensive diversification option if unwilling to exit overvalued tech and speculators; steady dividend and brand strength.
5.25% yield, 13x earnings; recent six-alarm distributor fire temporary headwind; CEO Mike Shoe solid; Cramer advocates averaging down despite near-term challenges.
